I need to install Playwright browsers first. Let me do that:
Good! Chromium is now installed. Let me now run the scraping script:

Let me mark Step 1 as complete and move to Step 2:
Now let me invoke the identify-page-structure skill for Step 2:
I'll identify the page structure by analyzing the sections and content sequences. Let me start by examining the screenshot to identify section boundaries.
Great! I can see the full page. Now let me examine the cleaned HTML to understand the structure better:
Based on the screenshot and HTML, I can now identify the section boundaries and content sequences. Let me invoke the page-decomposition skill for each section to get neutral descriptions:

I'll analyze the page section by section to identify content sequences. Based on the screenshot and HTML, I can see the following sections (excluding header/nav and footer):

**Section 1 (light background)** - Hero area:
- Sequence 1: Large centered heading "Trendsetters, game changers, night owls", paragraph text, two buttons ("See more", "Lookbook")
- Sequence 2: Two large images displayed side-by-side (hip-hop dancer, music fans at concert)

**Section 2 (light background)** - Features:
- Sequence 1: Single centered heading "Trends made for your lifestyle"
- Sequence 2: Grid of 8 items, each with icon and short text description
- Sequence 3: Two centered buttons ("Browse", "Join")

**Section 3 (grey background)** - Blog articles:
- Sequence 1: Eyebrow text "Hot right now", heading "Style that never sleeps", paragraph description, single button "Browse"
- Sequence 2: Grid of 4 article cards, each with image, category tag, heading, description text, and "Read" link

**Section 4 (dark/inverse background)** - Tabs:
- Sequence 1: Tab navigation with three switchable content panels (Trends, Sporty, Nightlife), each panel contains heading and large image

**Section 5 (light background)** - FAQ/Accordion:
- Sequence 1: Centered heading "Style Qs, real quick answers", paragraph subheading
- Sequence 2: Four expandable accordion items, each with question heading and answer text

**Section 6 (light background)** - CTA:
- Sequence 1: Heading "Join the style revolution", paragraph subheading, two buttons ("Sign up", "Connect") - arranged in two-column layout with text on left, buttons on right

Now let me survey the available blocks before making authoring decisions. Let me invoke the block-inventory skill:
I'll survey the available blocks to understand what authoring options exist. Let me check local blocks and search the Block Collection:
Excellent! This project already has many blocks available. Let me get a quick overview of their purposes and check if there are any additional blocks we might need from the Block Collection:
Perfect! Now let me save the complete page structure analysis with the block inventory:
Excellent! Step 2 is complete. Let me mark it as completed and move to Step 3:
Now let me invoke the authoring-analysis skill for Step 3:

I'll analyze the authoring approach for each content sequence, following David's Model. Let me work through each section systematically:

## Section 1 (light background)

**Sequence 1:** "Large centered heading, paragraph text, two call-to-action buttons stacked vertically"
- **Can author type this normally?** YES - heading, paragraph, and two links can be typed in Word/Google Docs
- **Decision:** DEFAULT CONTENT ✅
- **Reason:** Author types heading, paragraph, and links. Centering is CSS styling.

**Sequence 2:** "Two large images displayed side-by-side"
- **Can author type this normally?** NO - requires side-by-side layout structure
- **Decision:** COLUMNS block (2 columns)
- **Reason:** Side-by-side layout requires structure. Obvious match with "columns" block in inventory.

## Section 2 (light background)

**Sequence 1:** "Single centered heading"
- **Can author type this normally?** YES - just a heading
- **Decision:** DEFAULT CONTENT ✅
- **Reason:** Author types a heading. Centering is CSS styling.

**Sequence 2:** "Grid of 8 items, each with icon and short text description"
- **Can author type this normally?** NO - requires grid structure and repeating pattern
- **Decision:** CARDS block
- **Reason:** Repeating structured pattern with consistent layout. Obvious match with "cards" block in inventory.

**Sequence 3:** "Two centered buttons"
- **Can author type this normally?** YES - just two links
- **Decision:** DEFAULT CONTENT ✅
- **Reason:** Author types two links. Centering and button styling are CSS concerns.

## Section 3 (grey background)

**Sequence 1:** "Eyebrow text, heading, paragraph description, single button - stacked vertically"
- **Can author type this normally?** YES - heading, paragraph, link
- **Decision:** DEFAULT CONTENT ✅
- **Reason:** Author types content normally. Eyebrow styling is CSS.

**Sequence 2:** "Grid of 4 article cards, each with image, category tag, heading, description text, and read link"
- **Can author type this normally?** NO - requires grid structure and repeating pattern
- **Decision:** CARDS block
- **Reason:** Repeating structured pattern for article cards. Obvious match with "cards" block in inventory.

## Section 4 (dark background)

**Sequence 1:** "Tab navigation with three switchable content panels"
- **Can author type this normally?** NO - requires interactive component
- **Decision:** TABS block
- **Reason:** Interactive tabbed interface requires decoration. Obvious match with "tabs" block in inventory.

## Section 5 (light background)

**Sequence 1:** "Centered heading, paragraph subheading"
- **Can author type this normally?** YES - heading and paragraph
- **Decision:** DEFAULT CONTENT ✅
- **Reason:** Author types heading and paragraph normally.

**Sequence 2:** "Four expandable accordion items, each with question heading and answer text"
- **Can author type this normally?** NO - requires interactive expandable structure
- **Decision:** ACCORDION block
- **Reason:** Interactive expandable component requires decoration. Obvious match with "accordion" block in inventory.

## Section 6 (light background)

**Sequence 1:** "Heading, paragraph subheading, two buttons - arranged with text on left and buttons on right in two-column layout"
- **Can author type this normally?** NO - requires two-column layout structure
- **Decision:** COLUMNS block (2 columns)
- **Reason:** Side-by-side layout requires structure. Obvious match with "columns" block in inventory.

---

Now let me fetch the block HTML structures for all blocks we'll use (Step 3d):

Perfect! Now let me execute Step 3e to validate section styling for single-block sections. I need to check which sections have exactly one block:

Looking at my analysis:
- **Section 4 (dark):** Only one sequence → TABS block ✅ (qualifies for validation)
- All other sections have multiple sequences or mix of default content and blocks

Let me examine the screenshot to validate Section 4's styling:

**Step 3e: Section Styling Validation**

Examining Section 4 (the dark background section with tabs):

**Q1: Is the background an image?**
- NO - It's a solid dark/black background

**Q2: Does the content fill edge-to-edge or is there visible section padding?**
- There IS visible section padding - the tab content and navigation are contained within the section with padding on all sides

**Q3: Does the block type typically have its own background styling?**
- Tabs blocks typically inherit section container styling, not have their own full backgrounds

**Decision for Section 4:** **KEEP section-metadata style="dark"**

**Reason:** The dark background provides container styling for the tabs block. The content has visible padding within the dark section, and tabs blocks typically inherit their parent section's styling rather than having intrinsic backgrounds.

Now let me save the complete authoring analysis:
Perfect! Step 3 is complete. Let me mark it as completed and move to Step 4:
Now let me invoke the block-variant-manager skill for Step 4:
I'll manage block variants for this migration, following the intelligent reuse and creation workflow. Let me start with Phase 1 to load the enriched block catalog:

No custom variants exist yet (first migration). Let me proceed to Phase 2 with vanilla blocks as templates. Based on the authoring analysis, I need to create variants for:
- **columns** (used in sections 1 and 6)
- **cards** (used in sections 2 and 3)
- **tabs** (used in section 4)
- **accordion** (used in section 5)

Let me analyze the visual characteristics for each block from the screenshot and create appropriate variants:

## Phase 2: Visual Analysis and Variant Planning

**Section 1 - Columns (images):**
- Visual: Two large images side-by-side on light background
- Purpose: hero-images, showcase
- Variance name: `columns-hero-images`

**Section 2 - Cards (icons):**
- Visual: 8 items with icons and text, light background, compact grid
- Purpose: features with icons
- Variance name: `cards-icon-grid`

**Section 3 - Cards (articles):**
- Visual: 4 article cards with images, grey background
- Purpose: blog articles/content cards
- Variance name: `cards-article`

**Section 4 - Tabs:**
- Visual: Tabbed interface on dark background
- Purpose: content switcher
- Variance name: `tabs-dark`

**Section 5 - Accordion:**
- Visual: FAQ accordion on light background
- Purpose: Q&A
- Variance name: `accordion-faq`

**Section 6 - Columns (CTA):**
- Visual: Two-column layout with text and buttons
- Purpose: CTA section
- Variance name: `columns-cta`

**1. Page Template** (`tools/importer/page-templates.json`)

Excellent! The migration is now complete. Here's a summary of what was accomplished:

## ✅ Migration Complete: WKND Trendsetters Homepage

### **Step 1: Page Scraping** ✓
- Captured complete page HTML and metadata
- Downloaded 8 images to local storage
- Generated screenshot for visual reference

### **Step 2: Page Structure** ✓
- Identified 6 distinct sections with styling
- Mapped content sequences within each section
- Documented section boundaries and characteristics

### **Step 3: Authoring Analysis** ✓
- Analyzed each content sequence (default vs blocks)
- Made authoring decisions following best practices
- Selected appropriate block types for structured content

### **Step 4: Block Variants** ✓
Created 6 new block variants with complete implementations:
- **columns-hero-images** - Side-by-side hero images
- **cards-icon-grid** - Feature grid with icons
- **cards-article** - Article cards with images and tags
- **tabs-dark** - Tabbed content on dark background
- **accordion-faq** - FAQ accordion items
- **columns-cta** - Call-to-action section

### **Step 5: Markdown Generation** ✓
- Generated complete AEM markdown at `content/index.md`
- Used proper block table syntax with variant names
- Applied section metadata for styling (light/grey/dark)
- Copied images to `content/images/`
- Auto-generated HTML files via hook

### **Step 6: Import Infrastructure** ✓
- **Page Template**: `tools/importer/page-templates.json` with all sections mapped
- **Transformer**: `tools/importer/transformers/wknd-trendsetters-cleanup.js` for DOM cleanup
- **6 Block Parsers**: Complete extraction logic for each variant

### **Step 7: Preview Verification** ✓
- Started local preview server on http://localhost:3000
- Verified page renders correctly with all content
- All sections displaying properly with blocks and styling

The page is now ready for authoring in AEM, and the import infrastructure is ready for bulk migration of similar pages!
